data.content: This project uses MSTest v3 with the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`). For crash dump collection, use the `--crashdump` flag:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` with crash dump collection. For .NET test projects, crash dumps can be collected using the `--crashdump` option (available in Microsoft.Testing.Platform / MSTest v3+) or via the blame collector (`--blame-crash`).

Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` and MSTest 3.8.0, it uses the newer Microsoft Testing Platform. The crash dump collection can be enabled with `--crashdump` flag.

Let me run the tests with the appropriate crash dump option. For the Microsoft Testing Platform (which this project uses via `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`), the flag is `--crashdump`.


Now I'll execute the command.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-bc451c1e520b43319baf548b6d171841 && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content: The flag needs to be passed to the test platform via `--`. Let me fix that:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText: The `--crashdump` flag isn't being passed correctly — it's being forwarded to MSBuild instead of the test platform. With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, the `dotnet test` command should forward platform-specific options after `--`. Let me try with `-- --crashdump`.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-bc451c1e520b43319baf548b6d171841 && dotnet test -- --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command to run your tests with crash dump collection:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

The `--` separator passes `--crashdump` to the Microsoft Testing Platform (enabled by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` in your `.csproj`). If a test crashes the host process, a dump file will be written to the test results directory automatically.
